name: python_execute
description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed environment. KEY RULES: (1) DATA FILES — use the pre-defined variable DATA_DIR (already set, do not import os): df = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/iris/iris_combined.csv"). (2) NO DB CONNECTORS — duckdb, sqlalchemy, psycopg2 are NOT installed; use duckdb_query tool first, then rebuild: df = pd.DataFrame(result['rows'], columns=result['columns']). (3) PLOTLY — always set template='plotly_white' to avoid template errors: fig = px.bar(df, ..., template='plotly_white'); call fig.show() to render in canvas. (4) os module is blocked — use DATA_DIR instead of os.environ or os.path. Available libraries: pandas, numpy, scipy, matplotlib, plotly, openpyxl, pyarrow, statsmodels, scikit-learn, arch, riskfolio, networkx, xarray. Timeout: 60s.
